title: Lane E pickup brief for Furnace MB — Shaded hands off verified grounding (#37 + #22+23 PDR-058 optionality family); Shaded holds Lane A + WS-Z
body:
**LANE E PICKUP BRIEF — Group A graduations (#37 + #22+23 PDR-058 optionality family)**

For: **Furnace MB** (incoming). From: **Shaded Prowling Threshold** (claude / claude-opus-4-8 / `e526d7`). Owner-directed handoff 2026-05-29.

This brief is self-contained: it carries the live grounding I verified so you verify rather than rediscover. Re-confirm against the live repo anyway (`verify-dont-trust` — the source ledger was 83% wrong on Group C).

**WHERE THIS SITS.** Plan: `.agent/plans/agentic-engineering-enhancements/current/group-a-graduations-execution.plan.md` §WS-E. Lanes B/C/D landed (uncommitted, owner-approved prior session). Lane A authored + gated-green this session (Shaded; pending owner review). **Lane E is yours.** WS-Z convergence (shared indices) is Shaded's and depends on your Lane E + owner review.

**SANCTITY.** Lane E amends Core PDR-058 — agent-authored by YOU, owner-reviewed BEFORE finalising; NOT delegated to sub-agents (`subagent-practice-core-protection`; sub-agents may review, not author Core). Open your OWN claim on `PDR-058` + any rule files before editing (`register-active-areas-at-session-open`).

**LIVE GROUNDING (verified this session):**

1. **Surface 3 (Outcome Optionality) ALREADY EXISTS** fully in `PDR-058` §Decision (~lines 133–178): definition, impact, concrete shapes, diagnostic, cure (the falsifiability rule). Its closing paragraph leaves the *rule sibling* open for its own evidence trail and names the *don't-shoehorn-a-value-claim* candidate (2026-04-30) as its adjacent sibling that "may merge or remain distinct."
2. **Surface 4 (Sequencing Optionality) is NOT yet defined**, but PDR-058 §Decision lead (~lines 59–61) explicitly pre-architects it: *"adjacent candidates (interface optionality, **sequencing optionality**, scope optionality) may emerge and warrant their own surface."* #37 IS that surface — adding it is the extension the decomposition invited, not a rewrite.
3. **Register entries (`pending-graduations.md`):**
   - **#22** (~line 298) — *"Do not shoehorn value-claims into absent infrastructure"*: plans shouldn't invent brittle tests / fantasy operational protocols when the infra to know the claimed value doesn't exist. `target: rule:do-not-shoehorn-value-claims`, owner-gated.
   - **#23** (~line 307) — *"Outcome optionality standalone rule sibling"*: outcome must name a single observable signal; absent observing infra stated honestly, not simulated. `target: rule:outcome-optionality-falsifiability`, owner-gated.
   - **#37** (~line 497) — *"sequence-or-admit-not-doing — never use 'deferred' as a bare status; sequence (gate-relative or tripwire-based) or admit not-doing."* Owner quote: *"we never mark anything as deferred, we sequence things properly or we admit we are not going to do them. Sequencing can include 'when these specific tripwires fire'."* Bare-status `*: deferred` is the violation, NOT the word itself (fine as a modifier on a stated reopen tripwire).
4. **HOME CORRECTION (in plan, confirmed):** #37 → PDR-058 Surface 4, NOT `no-moving-targets-in-permanent-docs` (category error: no-moving-targets governs transient *values* in *permanent* docs; bare-deferred governs unsequenced *statuses* in *ephemeral* plans).

**MY RULE-vs-CLAUSE RECOMMENDATION (your call per `new-rule-vs-pdr-clause`; I'd verify then decide):**

- **Lean: graduate BOTH as PDR-058 §Decision clauses, NOT new always-on rule files.** Rationale: (a) `new-rule-vs-pdr-clause` preference order is existing-PDR-clause > new-rule; (b) PDR-058 already owns the substrate and pre-architected Surface 4; (c) the plan names "new always-on rules erode the directive context budget" as a risk + the 80k always-loaded budget; (d) **the active authoring-time enforcement layer already exists** — `.agent/rules/plan-body-first-principles-check.md` is an always-on rule firing *before authoring tests / implementations / doctrine* (3 clauses: shape / landing-path / vendor-literal). I had just read it when this handoff arrived: **it does NOT currently mention optionality.** A thin 4th-clause pointer there ("optionality-surface check → PDR-058 four surfaces") makes the doctrine FIRE without minting a new rule file (amendment to an existing rule = low cost, dodges the budget hit, and cures the passive-guidance gap — a PDR clause alone is passive, `passive-guidance-loses-to-artefact-gravity`).
- **Surface 3 merge insight:** #22 (don't-shoehorn) is the *infrastructure-absent special case* of #23 (falsifiability) — ONE cure, not two. PDR-058 Surface 3's cure ALREADY says *"if the infrastructure to observe the signal does not exist, the plan says so explicitly and ships the structural enforcement that does exist (the don't-shoehorn-a-value-claim substance)."* So graduation = confirm the merge, don't author two rules.

**ACCEPTANCE (plan §WS-E):** PDR-058 amended (Surface 3 rule-graduation confirmed; Surface 4 named with diagnostic + cure); enforcement homes authored (rule clauses and/or PDR clauses per your budget call); EACH surface captures the **falsifiable-tripwire-vs-vague-conditional distinction** — *"when the schema migration lands"* = legitimate sequencing; *"when we get to it"* = bare-deferred / imaginary-flow violation (cf. `feedback_simple_definite_no_imaginary_flows`). Owner sign-off recorded.

**AUTHORING DISCIPLINE:** PDR-058 already uses markdown path-links to rules/PDRs in its body, so `portability:check` (adapter-parity only) won't flag body paths — but prefer prose/identifier refs for portability-by-construction. Land amendments with an Amendment Log / Revision-history trail; Surface 4 goes in as a new §Decision subsection.

**VALIDATION:** `pnpm markdownlint:root`; `pnpm portability:check`; `pnpm practice:fitness:informational` (PDR-058 + any touched rule GREEN); `pnpm subagents:check` only if you mint rules with reviewer adapters (not expected).

**YOUR OWNED SCOPE:** `PDR-058-three-tier-optionality-decomposition.md` + any new `.agent/rules/*.md` (+ Claude/.agents/Cursor adapters if minted) + a thin clause in `plan-body-first-principles-check.md` if you take the pointer route. **DO NOT TOUCH** (other agents own these): `RULES_INDEX.md` / PDR decision-records `README.md` / `practice-index.md` / `pending-graduations.md` register / `repo-continuity.md` (WS-Z — **Shaded** owns all shared indices); `napkin.md` / `distilled.md` / the historical-napkin-synthesis report (**Highland Rising Squall** owns — claim `c2bb65a8`).

**WS-Z DEPENDENCY — REPORT BACK:** tell Shaded on this comms thread when (a) Lane E content lands, (b) owner approves it, and (c) whether you minted any new rules (so WS-Z adds the RULES_INDEX rows + adapter parity). WS-Z does not converge until your Lane E + Lane A are owner-approved and Highland's napkin rotation lands.
